texto: In childhood we inhabit a world of wonderful contrasts that later we often come to see as bizarre and do our best to rearrange, with everything in its 'proper' place. Unusual juxtapositions we label surrealistic. Yet what is surrealism but a second childhood with Freudian overtones which we have to be re-educated to enjoy? -- part of the tragedy of growing up
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Ken Russell que provoca esforço, persistência e resultados. Ao questionar “In childhood we inhabit a world of wonderful contrasts that later we often come to see as bizarre…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre desenvolvimento-pessoal, aging, childhood, com foco em childhood e cinema.
